content: i like the interface a lot, i think it's easy to use. the main reason i'm giving them 5 stars is for support. i lost access to my authenticator and account due to not using it for years. when i wanted back in and realized i was locked out, the support team worked hard to help me regain access, because of my own stupidity. i got to keep the one game i had actually bought at that point and my username, which i'm pretty attached to. say what you will, but the steam support team are awesome.

not sure why so many people have had issues with this app. i've used it since 2017 with no issues at all, on both android devices and ios/ipados. very useful in my case to catch deals and start remote downloads of new games or even to just patch them.

steam is honestly one of the greatest apps ever made, it has countless games and endless opportunities. the customer service is off the charts and amazing. i think steam has the best customer support i have ever experienced in my life. it's a lightweight, easy to manage app and is honestly superior to other gaming apps. thank you steam.

this is just an steam appreciation review, it is just so so much better that every other online game store, i dont want to use names here but most stores have so limited payment options but steam is compatible with literally anything and everything. dont even get me started on how good the customer service is, it always solves my problems within the same day. just one thing that can be improved is, when you sell an inventory item, the wait time of getting the money is very long.

literally the absolute best customer service in the world. my account was glitching so i couldn't buy games and i tried a few times and they responded within 30 minutes and the whole issue was resolved within 1.5 hours and they refunded the games and when i put in the wrong address it took my money but steam said it was declined so my banking account was glitching and later it was automatically refunded and i got the games. they basically have one of the best safety nets out of any gaming servic

the steam app for android is really good. but i think you need to add some features for a better experience like, add a wishlist counter for the steam android app and when we get back to the steam wishlist from a games page the scroller will not go down but stay at the exact position we left it before going to the game's page from the wishlist. please consider my suggestion.

works fine in my case, if you only need to check discount, shop some games, and more importantly...as your login key to anywhere. yeah sure it is not perfect. but we'll see later if their arm based project succeed, would they shift the focus to make their app perfect or not.

reason the overall rating is so low is because people usually just bother to make those when something wrong happens, not when it's running perfectly. my experience with the app is pretty good, though, since it does everything i want it to do. it does have improved a lot over the time.

i hadn't logged into steam for a while and had gotten a new phone/phone number (that i didn't update in my account). i also had lost my recovery codes and access to the authenticator. steam support was quick to respond and super helpful with getting me back into my account, removing my old phone number, and enabling me to log back in w/ the authenticator & update my account with my new phone number. happy and relieved to have access to all the games i'd purchased through there 😁 thanks, steam!

i had trouble paying for a bundle deal, and i thought i wouldn't be able to purchase it since the discount was over. it turns out steam support noticed my plight and allowed me purchase it with the discount still on. honestly best experience i have with this app

i have been trying to sign in for like 40 minutes. and it keeps saying "error" something something. first off all.. its not my internet. roblox plays smoothly but this app is not letting me sign in. and i have noticed that... i keep getting frickin logged out of my acc on pc... anyways. fix your ui and your app. ok, other than that, this app is good for playing games and stuff. so... 3 stars. ok. so after trying more times i finally signed in. that's why i'm changing it to 5 stars.. yey

great companion app to the desktop version. i just browse the steam store and buy my games remotely. no, you cant play games with this app. i dont think the majority (any tbh) of games are even designed to run on the android os.

has been using it for 3 years.. it is improving quite a lot. the important part, it can access steam wallet with it so im not missing out to buy a game that the discount almost gone in less than 5 minute. and i can top up with my local e-wallet.

my account got hacked and the only solution was to report it to the steam support. they banned the hacker instantly and i got my account back, and also once i forgot my password and they gave it to me instantly with only 1 verification check. i love yall steam support ppl yall r the absolute best❤️
the best of the best. the support squad is very helpful (as long as you provide information of course).lost my account but get it back . thank steam support(better than discord )

i only explained them what happened to my accaunt and they did not even hessitate to take action and help me i did not expect to see such a fast help from tickes ofc they have the best platform and i have no words to thenk them enaugh

it doesn't let me make a account i'm pretty sure i'm human, i've been happy, i've been in pain, i've cried, i've created art, i've even made friends, and yet i'm still kicking, so how come i can't play just because i clicked the button the wrong way?

love the app and customer service. had an issue with my steam deck delivery and steam resolved it promptly. have always been a pc gamer and will continue to use steam as long as they continue the stellar service, products, and service!

so i'm really only writing this review because of the fantastic customer service received in my service request. i don't necessarily have this app other than to monitor account access. however if you're downloading this app to play games this isn't the app you're looking for. thanks steam team for answering my questions 👌

the store under the tags section is a little broken visually. can't click anything after the tabs (global , friends tags, recommended). i see a list of tags as well as games overlayed, i can't click on either.
programmed great. after years of experience, steam is now available on smartphones. still, we don't have the "gaming" perspective.

no idea what these complaints are yapping about, i personally love this app myself. it's high quality updates consistently with my laptop account and has great features like the qr code sign in and more, must have for steam users who frequently play steam games.
i've used it now for over 5 years. i've never had an issue and it works great for me even when i lose my phone and get a new one. it's easy to transfer over steam support is the best by far!

absolute cinema steam is the best platform for games ever made. my accout was stolen and both the password and email was changed. i reached out to steam support and the problem was fixed in a matter of hours. thank you kelly from steam support.
great app, but i can't wat till this has fex and proton support, so that we finally can play windows pc games on our powerful android phones. i hope the support comes this year!
absolutely love steam. the sales are insane and valve in general is one of the most pro consumer entities in the gaming world. the app is great and easy to use.

just having this app with steam guard saved my account from getting hacked due to the "impersonating steam moderators and whatnots". just by having high security in my steam account i'm rating this five star.

this is the best gaming app ever. if you got a problem they will help you with the best of help they can do i had a problem with rust but the playtime was over 3 hours they made an exception and gave me in app money in steam ( which was the cost of rust and removed rust ) they are the best.

10/10 app it even let me download mods to my games without having to turn on my laptop plus is very simple and some of their games are very cheap thanks gabe for making a 10/10 app i didn't find a problem with it so far
great login feature. i can just scan the qt code and login without remembering my password. works great as my kids can never remember their password for anything.
it would be very nice if you guys updated the ui and overall user experience. the app feels outdated. yes, it works but it could be better.
an all around great experience. does what you need it to. just like the desktop version itself.
valve, when games in the app? :) honestly, gamenative and gamehub are already good enough of an experience for playing some steam games on android. we should have it already in this app! :-*
i love the steam app for my cellphone, it's very easy to use when i can't use my pc, it's makes things much easier and very practical.
very compact and useful. being able to view store and marketplace through this app is such a w, no need to open in the browser much smoother experience.
thank you steam. you really have a great service on helping me on getting my account back and i am really happy when i got my account back. so keep up the good work on the service
never had a problem with it. maybe those 1 star reviews are on old android which would have less of the libraries needed to run this app
no idea why the low rating. the experience is smooth af, and is similar and intiuitive to the pc steam program
anyone who has rated this 1 star and expected it to play their steam games is delusional. great app for browsing steam content without needing the desktop app.
steam mobile deserves an update of my review from 3 stars to 5. all previously stated issues are gone and the app feels complete.
i really wish that for some people.. in order to "play on your phone," you need to actually download it on your pc and then use the steam link.
i have only ever bought games through the mobile app. quick n easy, and immediately available!
great app keep it up but add some features like you can write reviews of products from mobile app.
must have if you want to get some good deals and it is also customisable
mobile version is not doing well currently but steam is still goated so anything less than 5 stars is unacceptable.
it was simple, nice and accessible. now, after the update,
it really helped me, they have the best account service, helped me reset my lost account password,thx ❤
best customer service ever.❤️❤️ i face some issues to login my account. steam support team fixed it with in few hour
people has very much honest to their heart among to the experience for such applications, as you can see rating on your screen.
why is the rating so low? it's new so it's ui is a work in progress.
i'm buying a steam deck but without and just on my phone the steam store runs smoothly and is very simple but i can't wait to get the steam deck so i can play games with steam
all the comments complaining are older comments. they fixed the app and its works fine. its still three stars because no one rates apps anymore.
the only ones who care about giving people a gaming experience without wanting to rob off our money with monthly subscriptions.
great app. store works great. authenticator works great. everything is great!
works fast, simple and convenient to use <3 on a samsung galaxy s22 ultra
great app, it lets you check your library and buy new games, and even remote download games.
it's a bit slower compared to the pc version but it allows me to do anything i couldn't do without it
i wasn't really gonna write anything about this app but man this app is the best like the jesus of apps cause the refund is so quick and the games in is so good thank you steam jesus
very good that instead of booting up your pc to see what's on the winter sale
i take back everything i said about steam; steam support is 1000 times better than xbox support.

steam has been a friend for a long time in my life, one of the best platforms. having all the games and programs available for sale remotely via the app is good. thankyou for your continuous support. oh and please give the developers of beamng drive a suitable amount of cash. that game has helped me a lot. and i enjoy it's constant and yet gradual development. leaps and bounds more like all the best. willvg95

ignore the low reviews. this app does not allow you to play purchased games on mobile devices! this is a companion app on mobile devices for the computer-based steam app. this lets you purchase games (for pc) away from home and utilize other steam functions while not at your computer. it's incredibly helpful if there's an exclusive sale for games and you aren't home. it's a great app.
great only way ĺt could be better is if i could play my games most won't be able to run on anyone's phone.
good solid app for usage and scrolling to gain games.
good way to browse sales and check if there's any news about new hardware when you are out and about

i don't know what people are complaining about, this app works fine. for clarity, no, this app doesn't allow you to play pc games on mobile, because that's not how software works. it's basically the app version of the steam website, with some additional features. the qr code login feature is very convenient, as i frequently need to sign into my steam account on other devices, and this app lets do it with the push of a button.
easy to use and best support team 💪
great app, authenticator code system is flawless for logging into new devices.

don't listen to the one star reviews. this app is basically just steam on a mobile device. no, it's not as good as the desktop app (nothing usually is), but for anything you'd realistically want to do it works just fine. want to do anything with your account or browse the steam store or use marketplace? you can do it here. video streaming has become way better, too. this is not the app that lets you play games - for that, go download the steam link or valve link app, forgot the name.
i genuinely love steam (valve) if there is anything or anyone looking out for the gaming community it's them. yeah sure they make profit from it, and rightly so. they provide
this app is absolutely peak! but i lost my guard code or whatever it's called, now i can't log in! please help me dev😭
a great way to access pc games from my phone and there's no ads!

the customer service is really good and very patient. i made many requests and they always replied quickly. i forgot my password and they helped reset it, and when i lost access to my authenticator, they removed it so i could log back into my account. their support made everything easy and stress-free.
been using steam for 10+ years, valve hasn't disappointed me once!
please support totp (time-based one-time password) as an alternative to steam guard authenticator.
pc games will be get low price... i love it very much ❤️
best customer support and service ever...!

i just want to put this out there i really hope steam wins this dumb lawsuit the moron did on steam. vicky shotbolt isnt an actual gamer xbox dlcs dont work for playstation or steam same with every console out there. prices are always affordable like the discounts steam does every season like they had the winter discounts. steam is such an amazing company and i hope steam just permanently ban the moron whos sueing steam. steam is the best customer support so helpful and hope vicki loses the case
its good too get new games on the go without the pc part

steam is the predominant pc gaming browser and it holds onto that title ferociously, i don't really have any problem with it, i just wish that i could find some games again, because steams library is massive and filled with constantly more content, its almost impossible to find some things a second time. the phone app is very useful if you want to check the new queue in your downtime. i would like it if i could play some games via it though. 9/10

i was one of steam's early adopters, many years ago. naturally, i *need* the steam mobile app to keep track of my wishlists for sale-notifications, and my friends that prefer steam's dm/chat system. the fact that it does all of this with relative ease and grace is testimony to the overall quality of steam itself. when there's a bug in a new feature, its usually handled in short order based on user feedback. if you're a pc gamer today, this is a must-have app.

i don't normally review apps, but steam has been my go to for gaming for a long time, and the app does not disappoint. you can browse & purchase games, put them on your wishlist, get notified when wishlist games go on sale, chat with steam friends (this feature is a little wonky with notifications, but works), and remotely install games to your pc if it's on wirh steam open, which is an excellent feature.

one of the classic pc game publishers with a massive repository of any kind of game you might be interested. a must have for pc-gaming. there is a tutorial (i think) when you initially run the game. (you can skip it) one important thing you should do is remind the overlay shortcut by going into your settings from the "file" menu near the top of the 'steam' window. (i don't think you have to hold alt down to show these) as i typically play first person shooters; and generally shift hastens..

i absolutely love and thankes steam. it has the best customer service especially when your account gets compromised like mine. they immediately got it back in less than a day. steam and his customer service are absolutely great and that's no doubt. i don't normally rate apps but steam did it so well that it got me here. thanks so much steam for getting my account back because i have spent quite some money on my account.

valve, please don't fall for the simplification trend like all the other apps, the current steam logo oozes style and is gorgeous. please keep it this way. the app is great, does exactly what it has to, the push notifications for steamguard are nearly instant and have been working flawlessly, the buttons in your library for game stats and all that are a bit small but the themselves work great. very big plus, all the tabs are very light on network usage, can be opened with the slowest connections

the steam app has been one of the most useful and convenient apps i have used for gaming. it's very easy to add things to your wishlist, view games, view your game playtime hours, view your profile, and etc. there are so many unique functions to the steam app. also, steam guard is very useful for keeping your account protected with an extra layer.

app is great for seeing what you already have in your inventory especially when trying to purchase games from another site online. you can verify if you already own a game but it would be very helpful if you could see all the dlc's you have within a game. currently i don't see an easy way to see what dlc's are included within a purchased game. you are able to see individual items purchased outside of a game but not if purchased with a bundle.

i love the steam app. it's literally like having steam on your device anywhere you go. the best part is when you get a notification about one of your wishlist items that are on sale. but anyway, the ui is easy to understand and navigate. linking your account is seamless, provided you know your information. it's a 5 star app for me.

the steam app makes it super easy to stay connected to my games and friends on the go. i can chat, check sales, browse the store, and even install games remotely — which is honestly such a useful feature. the interface is smooth and the new updates have made it even better. if you’re a pc gamer, this app is a must-have alongside your steam library.

ok i used to not be a fan of this app and it used to crash and only allow minimal account interaction and you had to be forced to use this to sell on the market your cards. but now i can access it easiest through my phone. i can answer people's messages i only know through steam. it let's me look up, purchase, comment, etc. basically everything aside from playing the games themselves. if you have steam this is a must have app.

yay! i can look at my library and wishlist whenever i want, so if i am with other people and am trying to think or reference a particular game of mine (but can't remember the name or be bothered to use my browser even if i do know it), it lets me more easily check it. and yeah. i think it used to be a lot less functional, but seems useful to me now. wish it would let me also access the collections in my library though. it's a real lot harder to find stuff without them.

good app, let's me support the creators behind the games whilst having incredibly good deals. quite easy to navigate as well. only thing is that maybe please add a feature where so you can run games that can support mobile devices there too, like terraria since but i don't know if i'm asking for too much so take this as you will. still a 5* for me tho!! keep up the good work and i hope you guys don't end up like spotify:3

i love steam mobile app, it's very useful for someone on the go who needs to be able to interact with multiple accounts. however, i've encountered an issue wherein paypal + steam don't function together properly, or at all really. it's probably on my end but if not then please fix!

if you're a hardcore, long-term (15+ years) steam user like myself this app is an absolute necessity. it's insanely useful and in a central requirement, especially because of the two-factor authentication it provides. one of the only complaints i have is that the store/community browsing is too similar to a web browser experience. which makes it not an improved mobile experience. if they cleaned up how you browse through store community it would be 0erfext. not enough to detract from it though.

it does what it needs to do. i mainly have this for steam guard (the notification always pops up immediately), and a quick notification whenever a game i want goes on sale. you obviously can't play games on it, but mobile gaming isnt exactly what people want now. it really does what it needs. steam chat is a nice thing to have inapp, but it also has a separate app, which is also downloadable. personaly i only use steam chat to spam steam emotes, normally discord suffices but it is always a good idea to have multiple lines of communication.

a nice, clean interface that lets you see exactly what you'd expect. you can see what your friends are playing, message them, and do a lot of other things that are just convenient to have on the go. also, the authenticator makes your account basically impossible to be breached by a third party or someone who wants to steal your information. so, basically, if you have steam, this is a nice way to be able to access the social side of the platform from wherever you are, and i recommend having this.

using my account 8 years by now, steam mobile autenticator really does make a real difference when it comes in security. *edit: at some point, the application doesn't allow me to remove review's filters(can't see it on my device) and some details can't be seem using android 4.4.2 kitkat at 720x1280 screen, even sideways. when i try to add someone using a quick-invite option, the page won't load, games can't be added as well. i'll appreciate if those minimal problems could be fixed. thank you.

i don't understand what other people is talking about, it's not confusing at all if you actually try to understand how it works. it's easy to understand,navigate and great for finding games while you're not at your computer/laptop. please ignore the other people's stupidity and ignorance. i can pay,add to cart,add money to steam wallet and buy dlc just fine. it also doesn't have network issues.

a functional app for managing your steam library away from your computer so you can play them on your computer (not chromebook) for later. it's also great for keeping your account secure with steam guard. however, this mobile app is not made to let you play games wherever, it's for buying games, managing your library, inventory, chatting on forums, chatting with firends, and protecting your account on the go. that is its purpose and it serves its purpose perfectly.

helpful a lot to keep me updated for the games that being on sale. btw, a minor problem i encountered is when i was on the developer/publisher page, filter the games based on discount, i clicked a game, and then back to the page, the filter got reset, hence i needed to scroll back to the top to apllied the discount filter again. i wish it gets fixed asap, thanks.

people writing the negative reviews don't understand that this mobile app isn't for playing games on mobile, playing pc games natively like that on your mobile device is impossible. this app is meant to browse and manage things about your steam account, such as authencation and friends. yes you can browse the store and workshop for games, but the only purpose of this app is do set up things you can do on your pc later. not meant to play games. 5 stars.

leaving this to boost reviews. it's just steam on your phone. people need to get over themselves. you can buy games and browse games to see what you want from your phone. it literally does exactly what it's intended to do. obviously if you want the mod page you should do that on your pc anyways cuz that's how you're gonna play it. duh.

great app, i love it. not the most good looking of course, but it's functional and takes just a few mbs of storage. you get steam guard for 2fa, confirmations for trades, the store page, the community hub and you can chat but it's pretty rough (that's why they have a different app for the full mobile steam chat experience). recommended for increased account security and a steam version in your pocket for whatever reason you might need that (without the games of course, duh).

i have used this app for 4 months now and love it. i had trouble 'error's logging in first time i used it but that was because you have no choice but to agree to the permissions it prompts, so i uninstalled and reinstalled, confirmed the permissions and now it works great. i use the chat so i don't have to chat on my computer while in game and can just pause and pick up my phone to send msgs to my friends on steam instead. fast, store loads and does everything i need it to.

steam has consistently proven itself as the ultimate platform for pc gaming. the user interface is intuitive, the library management tools are seamless, and the frequent sales make building a massive collection both easy and affordable. community features like workshop support, cloud saves, and game hubs create an immersive experience far beyond just launching games.

please, make the corners of the app logo bit rounded. yes, it is not shown in the app list, but wherever other places, it is so annoying. and also put a button to start scanning a qr code , when i want to just go guard tab, it is accessing my camera. and when i tap my device in authorized devices it is showing 'oops, something went wrong', please, fix it also. then adding community server browser to the mobile app would be great!

very good and useful for your phone. it runs smoothly for the most part, one of the few issues i do have however, is that sometimes the app cuts things off. like certain blocks of text, there is a simple way to fix this though which is just turning to landscape or zooming out if the particular page allows. turning on the steam guard mobile authenticator also works fine as well, i have had no issues with it. if you have steam on your pc this is a great companion for your mobile device.

this app is spectacular (in any gadgets) but why can't i find my classmate in the "add friend" section? i searched exactly his name, the capital letters and numbers. it only shows other. and why is "rules of survival" costs around ₱99.00? i recommend a free version of free games and let the price stay for those who want to buy packs instantly. i will still give 5/5 because of the great performance i have experienced.👏👏👏

this is the most painless 2fa implementation i've ever seen. and it asks you for confirmation on trades/selling items, giving you one last chance to double-check everything to prevent scams or gross undersells on items. you can chat with people while at work or something so you can figure out who's down to play tonight. this entire app is a massive quality-of-life improvement for any steam user. my only issue is: sometimes your read messages don't sync up with the desktop client.

a very useful and helpful app that's very good for adding friends to your friends list and managing your steam account. my only issue so far has been when trying to add friends where when i clicked add friend it just went back to the same page although this only occasionally happens and doesn't interfere all that much with the experience of the app.

at this point, i think i've decided to give my money to steam for anything gaming. haven't had a new console for a long time and probably won't get a new one ever again. i blame pc gaming, especially steam. it's just very convenient with crazy sales. i got the app just for the steam authenticator to guard my steam library, not that anyone would ever want it. i can also browse around for more games to buy. 10/10

steam's support team is the best. my account got compromised (hacked) 2-3 hours ago and i thought it was gonna be a tiresome process to get it back but naw. steam's team responded promptly and helped me get access to my account with ease. i thought it was gonna take a day or two to get my issue resolved. i was really devastated and stressed but right now i'm all smiles.

this app is good for gamers to find games for free but also find games that cost money. i recommend it because i have used it over a year now and it has never failed me. to buy a game you just got to buy a steam gift card and put it in your steam wallet. once that is done have a great time making friends ingames and trade or just talk!!!

so, guys: it's a bloody good, moist app. however, there's one important element missing: steam stats. when i want to have a quick look at steam's current top 100 most played games etc, i have to do it through chrome. if this was in the app, just like full-fat steam, it'd be basically perfect, son. thank you.

brilliant app, to the people who are complaining about moving between the app and browser to retrieve their code, well you have im guessing about 5 minutes to use the same code, because when i switch between app and browser to go to my email to get the code, you must login and the code is requested again, do not switch back to email just proceed using the first code, it will work.

it's a great way to protect your account, check steam sales, receive steam sales notification for a video game that you want, it's a convenient way to check out new video games available on steam without turning on your pc, receive friend text messages. and you can also redeem codes like your steam wallet code easily. i would recommend this app for anyone who has money in their account because it has great security and it's quite convenient.

i just recently started using steam fully 100% since august\september 2024 last year and it's actually one of the best marketplace clients to browse games! no, you can play games on there outright, that's what steam remote play is for. my overall experience with steam has been great! games download to your pc okay when you purchase, chat with friends and more. that's it and honestly i couldn't ask for more than what it offers right out of the box!

used this app for convenience since i can open it anywhere, and constantly logging in browser is annoying. i did encounter some minor bugs but they're not that bad to fully ruin my experience using the app. i haven't yet encountered any issues with app purchases or steam wallet top ups, but i hope i don't whenever i do so in the future.

a must have for avid steam users like myself. needed for two factor authenticator and confirming trades. you can access basically everything on the mobile version that you can on the pc version. allows for faster trading as well as added security. overall i haven't run into any outstanding issues with it so it's all good for me.
it has everything tf2 half life.. though gabe can't count to 3 it's ok there's still a lot of games that's so good

steam is great for many reasons, but the most important one is their extremely helpful staff team. i lost my account around 1 year ago and managed to get it back without any payment details or proof, the staff thankfully found my provided information sufficient and i got my account back. it also has no gift card regions so you dont have to go through the boring part of getting a gift card for germany, france, italy ect, their cards are international. their staff team is also very quick to reply.

woah one of the best game seller applications and the customer service. recently i have faced an issue with my account login in both epic and steam i didn't get any help from epic but steam replied in less than 8 hours. it's amazing i feel very safe here in steam

people are describing a loop of verification and yes it is a very bad bug bit it can be easily avoided if you simply set up your steam account before you download the app. i uninstalled steam and simply set up account on browser then downloaded steam again.

personally no problems at all the account recoveries are very expanded to save your account as much as they can even though you give a small amount of information the payment for games is professional nothing wrong love it you must download!😃

giving 5 stars, but also reporting a long living bug. when going to community -> workshop, there is sometimes a search bar ui, and other times it simply does not exist and even if i refresh the app (heck, even deleting cache data) it doesn't fix it. eventually the search bar reappears hours later. not a huge issue, but for someone who is active in workshop communities and not always at his pc, this can feel like a pain in the steam pipe (lol for the pun) 5 star because still amazing service. 😊

i want say thank you steam support for saving my account today. lately,i feel something wrong why i can't log in. it's because after i searched about it and found that my account got hacked. without wasting time,i contacted steam support and yes,they helped solving problem that what was mine. i really really appreciate your service, support 🙏🏻 wish you have great day everyday.

an excellent forum to have access to some of the best deals in pc gaming ever! organize all your games in one place, one library, steam games or 🚫. chat forums, game guides, ratings, and much more. it's free. can't beat that, right?

my only gripe is the log in process. thats steams fault and not the app. pc gaming with iphone frustrations. i love the selection, and after finally getting an account set up and passwords saved i can play the stuff that takes way too long to pirate from the internet and i can play all the need for speeds without figuring out what console to plug in. upgrading monitors and juicing the laptop to take advantage of better graphics potential with pc and avoiding console bottle necks.

i haven't had the issues with this app that others have, so i cannot speak to that. at the beginning i had some run ins with safety measures that were put in place, to protect my accout, but no problems for the last few years. if i was to say that this app was missing one thing, i would ask that it could be made into a mobile games platform in is own right. one that could play games from my account, that are available on steam and on mobile. although i understand that pigs will fly before that.
